likes:
While working for ITC limited FMCG verticals, namely Cigarettes, Foods and Personal care. 
The easiest to work for is cigarettes as it needs not to be sold. It gets off the shelf itself. And workload is minimal. 
Then comes Personal care which has least value contribution as well as less work pressure too. Toughest division to work for is undoubtedly the Foods division wherein you have a very very vast product portfolio and company tracks billing of all at outlet level, which makes it even worse. 
If you get posted in a tourist visiting town, you'll have a lot of company official visits for which you'll have to decorate the market inside out with ITC products, which is really tough. 
Therefore, it isn't as lustrous as it seems from outside.
dislikes:
Company doesn't provide good training and expects the employee to learn all by himself. 
There are so many company specific softwares and apps which we have to learn all by ourselves. 
Then if you get into foods. You'll face a lot of problems as over 250 products are there in the arsenal, which they expect that we must sell and not even lose out on even a single sku.

likes:
Brand is good. Very Wide range of product portfolio. People perception is very optimistic when you say you work for ITC. On time salaries... Good network.
On the job training is very good and knowledge on FMCG working will be improved. Travelling allowance, Sampling reimbursement.
dislikes:
Culture is very poor, you hardly get to see your immediate boss because of the nature of the work - on field sales. You have to be very calm and respect your colleagues and bosses with 'SIR'. Also as an area executive you hardly get to know what's happening in your company. HR is almost useless... HR hardly connect with employees to get feedback. Lot of work. Most of our targets depend on other employees productivity. Technical skills will go down... very down. You'll make love with EXCEL ONLY.  Frequency of getting promotions is very low. No team outings, no team lunches, no entertainment. Just come to your work... Be a ROBOT... Go to home. 
Full on field sales role. Health, hygiene and lifestyle will be disturbed for sure.

likes: ITC has given me growth in terms of every aspect, but on account of certain compromises. Working for ITC is prestigious among all, but due to recent covid situation has worsen the work culture. You name it any division or category, everyone got hit. ITC is not realising same and changing its way of process to achieve targets. They have become more aggressive and aren't listening to any ground level team. They need to understand that balancing mental pressure is as important as achieving given targets and agendas. They need to value human resource.
dislikes:
Every business has advantages and disadvantages. All you have to do now is identify your grey areas and collaborate with the company. This firm has provided me with a wealth of business expertise.
Pros
Teammates are helpful,
In terms of process, JIT is in sync,
Day to day learnings,
Multitasking, Etc.
Cons 
Salary is a sensitive subject for me since, despite putting in a tremendous amount of effort and hopping from one place to another, I believe I'm underpaid.
Appraisals are tainted because of involvement from others, and they are not based on actual performance.
Politics is a natural part of business and every Organisation  is faces it.
Among all of these obstacles, I believe that work-life balance should be restore with good direction and certainty.

likes:
ITC is a place for learning and gather knowledge.
Job security is very good no one would kick someone out except for policy violations.
dislikes:
Salary increments even for outstanding performance itself will be 5.5% of monthly Salary minimum is 2.5%.
 
Promotions are given once in 4 years on an average for lower grade/level employees, only if the boss likes you not based on your output.
Even if you complete B.E./B.Tech after joining it will not be considered for promotion.
Appraisal methods are worst, they dont even tell exactly what the employee has to improve in the comments.
Career growth for persons with a diploma is worst ever, diploma persons would retire from job even before reaching assistant manager level (Grade F).
Gross CTC will be high but your actual take home would be 70-75% of gross CTC rest will be variable and PF.
Work pressure is very high and increasing, work culture is also rapidly degrading in own manufacturing units during recent years. 
They don't show/give you the exact/full HR policy to employees at all. Even if you ask they will show the claim & reimbursement policies only.
They will not open tell the requirements for promotions, sponsorship for higher studies, long unpaid leave for higher studies without sponsorship, consideration for transfer to other departments/divisions.
No open forum discussions for issues highlighted by employees. They will not tell what feedbacks had been received exactly. But they will show some points for which they took action and create an image like they took action on all feedbacks.
Main thing is that if you get a good manager you would get a better appraisal ratings and faster promotions otherwise you will be struck forever.
Because there are a lot of employees even without a single promotion in 10 years also.

What do you like about working at ITC?*
Based on my understanding of my knowledge, here are some of the things that people like about working at ITC:
Job security: ITC is a well-established company with a long history of success. This gives employees a sense of job security, knowing that they are likely to have a stable income and benefits.
Good pay and benefits: ITC offers competitive salaries and benefits, including health insurance, retirement plans, and paid time off.
Opportunities for growth: ITC encourages employees to develop their skills and advance their careers. There are many opportunities for training and development, as well as promotions within the company.
Work-life balance: ITC strives to create a healthy work-life balance for its employees. This includes flexible work hours and a supportive work environment.
Good company culture: ITC has a positive and supportive company culture. Employees feel valued and appreciated, and they are proud to work for the company.
Challenging and rewarding work: ITC employees have the opportunity to work on challenging and rewarding projects. This allows them to use their skills and knowledge to make a difference in the world.
Of course, no company is perfect, and there are some downsides to working at ITC as well. Some of the cons that have been mentioned in reviews include:
Slow career growth: Some employees feel that it is difficult to advance their careers at ITC. This is due to the fact that the company is hierarchical and there are limited opportunities for promotion.
Long hours: Some employees work long hours, especially during busy periods. This can lead to work-life imbalance.
Bureaucracy: ITC can be a bureaucratic company, which can make it difficult to get things done.
Overall, ITC is a large and complex company with a variety of pros and cons. Whether or not you would enjoy working there depends on your individual preferences and priorities.

employment_type: TMD - FMCG - Foods and Personal Care Department
likes:
Only thing good about them is their Brand Identity they have made out their Cigarettes and Hotels business.
* SUMMARY *
1. Company hiring MBAs for under-grad based roles, even a BA graduate can work in that role.
2. SALARY STRUCTURE - Salary structure is exploiting. Salaries are literally peanuts, TE - travel expenses compensation are worst in the industry, I mean who pays Rs100 TE for travelling and Rs75 for Food, where fuel rates are Rs100/litre. This becomes Rs300 (travel +food) after you become sales officer, but process to get it passed is still gross.
3. Work profile - work is that of a person who can be called a Salesman, if your parents have paid for your MBA to be salesman and nothing else, join ITC and good luck!
4. Work culture - Bureaucratic work culture.  Babu / Saahab / Sirji, these are terms which your managers and super managers devote their seniors with. So, basically you are working for a FMCG company which acts like a government dept in a old traditional bank/PSU organisation. 
5. HR working - HR policies are just for paperwork, you won't see a reflection on ground. HR has no role to play, reflect their role to bring harmony and organisational work in this company, HR will do nothing if your manager harass you.
dislikes:
1. WHY ITC IS HIRING ST/SO, AMT , AUTs in bulk every year -
They are hiring Tier 2 and Tier 3 MBAs in bulk. Thats good for ITC and not for you, bcs this gives them bargaining power in the jobs market, they fool MBAs in the name of ITC's brand name.
Always remember,
A HIGH HIRING RATE = A HIGHER ATTRITION RATE.
which is not good sign for the work culture of an organisation.
2. SALARY -
They are exploiting MBA graduates for under-graduation based roles and salaries, else who hires a MBA and a third party TL/SO/Supervisor at same salary level ? ref: Rs25K pm.
Only an exploiting organisation which doesn't cares for your professional growth and an employee who doesn't cares for his/her career trajectory would work at such low level salaries.
3. ASK YOUR CAMPUS TO DENY -
If they ever come to your college for Sales Trainee / Sales officer profile hiring, ask your campus recruitment cell for a big no! There is Nestle, Mondelez, HUL/P&G hiring at tad better salaries for same roles, at least you'd be qualified for a salary growth negotiation after working with Nestle and HUL etc. (FYI these companies offer upto 7L pa starting CTC and ITC hires you for 3.7-4L pa)
4. ROLE AND RESPONSIBILITY STRUCTURE -
At ITC positions are like, 
Tier 3 : They hire MBAs as Sales trainee, statutorily promote them to Sales officer next year (waste of your time, so keep stuck here), and Then promote them to Area Executive (after 2 yrs of working.)
SALES TRAINEE AND SALES OFFICER are profiles added just to cut down company cost, so they can at least get you stuck with them for the next 3 yrs, and at a lower salary than industry by default.
Tier 2 : They hire AMT - Area manager trainees from Tier 2 colleges, and put them to handle a team of Area Executives in a defined area of city (usually handled by 14-15 distributors), mind you an AMT - Area Manager handles a less denser area of 3-5 distributors at Nestle and gets paid more than an AM - Area manager at ITC.
5. WORK LIFE BAL. - OVERBUDERNED.

likes:
Good brand Indian company with core values. Job security is 100%. Good medical facilities. You can not assume permanent location as company can transfer any time as per requirement so be flexible all the time. 
Can get good learning and experience from seniors. Work culture is normal. Long working hours as per requirement but not in routine. 
Only drawback is very low salary hike annualy even less than market standard. Promotions can get in 3-4 years gap or as per your bonding with reporting manager. 
Overall very good company to work with normal work life balance and even can plan your retirement if you have not any higher wishes of self growth.

likes:
Only brand name is the attraction for joining at ITC. Initially, you join as Intern (2 months, it does not count towards work experience) with moderate stipend and TA. Then you get permanent job role of Sales Trainee/Assistant Under Training. For Sales Trainee, after 1 year, Sales Officer and after 2 years, Area Executive. After that the growth is stagnant.
For AUT, it might take 1 - 2 years to become Assistant Branch Manager.
ABMs can switch to other teams, but SO or AEs have only one direction to move.
Job security is there at ITC.
You will get to learn a lot, often in the painful ways. Because you have to do everything alone. Your mentor will be busy handling sales and may not be meeting you frequently. So it makes you self-dependent.
dislikes:
There is really no reason to join ITC these days, if you do not crave for the brand name or only if you want to continue in sales profile till you retire. As an employee, you will not find much dignity. Sales Trainees are treated like prostitutes. 
ITC does not have a proper training department. After 5-6 months even when you have had enough training, you are asked to continue the same work. Also there is a perception gap - Branch Manager will expect you to understand the strategic and operational levels whereas your mentor Area Executive will expect you to sell products in the market tagged with a salesman.
The initial salary given to Sales Trainees are 3.6 LPA to 4 LPA.  After 1 year it is 5.3 LPA and after 2 years, it is 7 LPA. Salary is very low considering that it is hardcore sales job and the amount of travel and also according to market benchmark.
Work life balance is frustratingly low here. You may have to work till 11PM at night.
